paragraph: Single-family homes in Rutherford are moving at a near-perfect 1.00 sale-to-list ratio with half the homes selling above asking and a median of 24.5 days on market, which tells you this is a competitive but balanced market where priced homes are moving steadily. The median sits at $858,000 with a range from $525,000 to $1,600,000, giving you genuine variety depending on neighborhood and condition. Multi-family properties follow a similar pattern at a $897,500 median, though they linger slightly longer at 51.5 days on market, suggesting buyers are more selective when comparing two-unit investments. Condos and townhouses occupy a distinctly different price tier at $349,000 median with a 29-day pace and a sale-to-list ratio right at 1.00, making them the entry point for buyers who want lower carrying costs and no land maintenance. Co-ops sit at the bottom with a $202,550 median and only six sales over the seven-month window, indicating limited inventory in this category. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the investment potential of a multi-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
